title: Bash Script Template
when_to_use: When building any reusable Bash automation script

steps.title: Shebang Line
steps.tip: This tells the system to use Bash. Without it, the script may run with a different shell

steps.title: Usage Function
steps.instruction: Add usage function: usage() { echo 'Usage: $0 -t target'; exit 1; }
steps.tip: Always provide a usage message. Users shouldn't have to read the code to use the tool

steps.title: Parse Arguments
steps.instruction: Parse arguments: while getopts 't:h' opt; do case $opt in t) TARGET=$OPTARG;; h) usage;; esac; done
steps.tip: The colon after 't:' means -t requires a value. No colon for flags like -h

steps.title: Validate Input
steps.tip: Always validate. Never assume the user provides correct input. Fail gracefully with helpful messages

steps.title: Create Output Directory
steps.tip: -p creates parent directories and doesn't error if it exists. Timestamped dirs prevent overwrites

steps.title: Define Functions
steps.instruction: Add your functions: recon(), enumerate(), report()
steps.tip: Each function should do one thing well. Name them clearly. Pass parameters explicitly

steps.title: Execute and Redirect
steps.instruction: Call functions in order and redirect output to files
steps.tip: Use tee to both display and save: command | tee output.txt. Helps with real-time monitoring
